items.name: A car
items.source: Water Footprint Calculator. Note: Lifecycle assessment includes water used in production but not scope 2/3.

items.name: A piece of paper
items.source: Water Footprint Calculator. Note: Lifecycle assessment includes water used in production but not scope 2/3.

items.name: An average American shower
items.source: 2016 Residential End Uses of Water. Note: includes only water used in shower itself, not to create/power the shower (scope 2/3).

items.name: A cotton t-shirt
items.source: Water Footprint Calculator. Note: Lifecycle assessment includes water used in production but not scope 2/3.

items.name: A teaspoon of water
items.source: US Liquid Measurements. Note: This is the actual water content of a teaspoon.

items.name: A cup of coffee
items.source: Water Footprint Network (140L per 125ml cup). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain. Includes coffee bean cultivation and processing.

items.name: An egg
items.source: Water Footprint Network (196L per egg). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain. Includes chicken feed and processing.

items.name: A pair of jeans
items.source: Water Footprint Network (7,982L for 1kg denim). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain.Includes cotton growing, dyeing, and manufacturing.

items.name: A chocolate bar (100g)
items.source: Water Footprint Network (1,700L per 100g). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain. Includes cocoa cultivation and processing.

items.name: A hamburger (quarter pound beef)
items.source: Water Footprint Network (15,415L per kg beef, ~113g patty). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain.Includes feed crop irrigation and processing.

items.name: A glass of wine (125ml)
items.source: Water Footprint Network (109L per 125ml glass). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain.Includes grape cultivation and wine production.

items.name: A slice of bread
items.source: Water Footprint Network (40L per 30g slice). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain.Includes wheat cultivation and baking.

items.name: An apple
items.source: Water Footprint Network (70L per 150g apple). Note: WFN uses a blue + green + grey water methodology, not a 1/2/3 scope assessment but attempts to assess water consumption along the entire supply chain.Includes orchard irrigation.

chatGPTEstimates.info: Estimated U.S. Average: 29.3 GPT-3 queries per 500 ml (Ren et al. 2023). Note: this takes into account first, second and third-scope water use, including water used in cooling, constructing and making the data center and its components.

Google Cloud Report on Measuring the Environmental Impacts of AI: 0.26 milliliters (or about five drops) of water per Gemini query. Note: this is an estimate for the water used per inference, only taking into account first-scope use for cooling a data center

Altman's own estimate from blog post "The Gentle Singularity" suggests a single query uses 1/15 of a teaspoon of water. Note: this is only the water used for the query itself, and does not take into account the cooling of data center, model training or any further-scope water uses.
